metrics.narrative: Zycus quantified 8-10% sourcing-cycle-time and PR-to-PO conversion savings during the introductory call on 1 Feb 2025, and Sayantan Mukherjee's team confirmed scale drivers (over 400 active vendors, 2,000+ POs per month, ~150 RFPs per month) that make automation valuable. However, this is still a rep-supplied estimate, not a buyer-confirmed business case: the CFO explicitly wants ballpark implementation cost and ROI figures for the 13 Jul 2026 meeting (raised by Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026), and that quantified case has not yet been delivered.

sources: Zycus X Bandhan Bank Quick meeting, 2026-07-02
champion.narrative: Nishan Kumar (DVP, Strategy & Program Management) is explicitly named as champion ('My Champion (Nishan)', Next Step entry 23 Jun 2026) and has demonstrated real advocacy: he personally coached Zycus on how to win the CFO over, told the team their product/technology presentation was rated #1 and 'a wow factor' (2 Jul 2026 call), proactively arranged the second CFO/CIO session, and is coordinating meeting logistics directly with the deal owner. He is currently the sole confirmed thread into the buying committee.

competition.narrative: Zycus was one of roughly 20 vendors evaluated, narrowed to 6 by 9 Apr 2026, and now sits in the final 3 as of the 17 Jun 2026 demo round. Early-stage comparators named directly by Sayantan Mukherjee were SAP Ariba, Ivalua and Coupa (7 Feb 2025); later internal Bandhan materials also benchmarked against GEP Worldwide and Oracle Fusion (10 Apr and 17 Apr 2026). The sole scoring gap versus the surviving field is the absence of India BFSI implementation experience, explicitly flagged by Nishan Kumar on 2 Jul 2026 as the reason a rival with local banking implementation credentials could still win despite Zycus's #1 product/technology ranking.

sources: Discovery Call: Bandhan Bank x Zycus, 2025-02-07
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and consistent across a year of engagement: Sayantan Mukherjee described a fully manual, email-based RFI/RFP and vendor-negotiation process with no formal scorecarding (7 Feb 2025 call), and the broader team confirmed manual invoicing, poor vendor onboarding, and no real-time visibility or reporting (17 Apr 2026 and 17 Jun 2026 sessions). RBI regulatory pressure for vendor performance and SLA management is repeatedly cited as the forcing function.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, order-form, or contracting mechanics have been discussed in any call or note through 8 Jul 2026. This is expected at this stage (Shortlisted, pre-CFO-decision) and is not yet a genuine risk, but it is a real knowledge gap: nothing is known yet about signatory authority, paper templates, or timeline once the CFO gives direction on 13 Jul 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: Rajeev Mantri is named and confirmed as the ultimate approver, sitting on the 5-member technical committee alongside the CIO and other senior leaders (per the 10 Apr 2026 call). Zycus has never had direct contact with the CFO or the CIO; the first direct engagement is the confirmed 13 Jul 2026 Mumbai presentation. Access to power exists structurally (the CFO already scored Zycus #1 on product/technology via the panel process) but is entirely indirect until that meeting happens.

decision_process.narrative: The process is unusually well documented: RFP received 5 Feb 2026, extended and submitted by 17 Feb 2026, evaluated down from roughly 20 vendors to 6 (9 Apr 2026), then to 3 finalists after tailored demos (17 Jun 2026 in Kolkata). A 5-member technical committee (Rajeev Mantri, an F&A head, a procurement head, and IT leadership) scores vendors; the surviving 3 now proceed to a 45-minute CFO/CIO business-credentials session on 13 Jul 2026, expected to precede commercial negotiation.

sources: Bandhan Bank <> Zycus - Next stage update, 2026-04-10
Zycus X Bandhan Bank Quick meeting, 2026-07-02
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicitly documented: application effectiveness against current pain points, cost/efficiency gains (days and headcount reduced), depth of AI/automation, and future product roadmap, plus a formal weighted scoring sheet, per Nishan Kumar's 10 Apr 2026 briefing. The 2 Jul 2026 call confirmed three named scoring pillars: product, implementation, and technology, with Zycus ranked #1 on two of the three.

The decisive CFO/CIO gate is not until 13 Jul 2026, and commercials/RFQ are explicitly sequenced only after that session (Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026: 'RFQ thing will happen post our call with CFO and panel'). The recorded close date cannot be met this cycle on current pace, though the deal itself is healthy and actively advancing.

flags: Convert champion Nishan's advocacy into CFO sponsorship: deliver a crisp, confident pitch that proves Zycus can execute in India BFSI despite no prior local banking implementation, using Malaysia/Philippines/Singapore banking entry as proof points.

Economic Buyer not yet engaged; first contact at CFO meeting week of 13 Jul is high-risk (no warm-up, no sponsorship established). Mitigation: secure 15-min CFO pre-brief through Nishan to surface CFO's top concerns before formal meeting.
No commercials exchanged; commercial template promised by buyer 23 Jun but not yet received as of 7 Jul.

Implementation credibility gap is sole blocker and CFO-level red flag. Nishan stated 2 Jul: 'Only red flag on your presentation... implementation is where you got lower scores.' Failure to name India BFSI partner by CFO meeting will lose deal to GEP. 6 days remaining to onboard partner.
Close date 8 Jul predates CFO meeting week of 13 Jul; date will slip ~5-7 days post-CFO sign-off.
Neutralize the sole scored weakness (implementation credibility) by naming and rehearsing a specific India-banking delivery partner before 13 Jul; product and technology are already won.

Convert Nishan Kumar from a neutral, all-vendors process coach into an actively partisan internal champion by arming him with a crisp, quotable ROI figure he can carry into the CFO room ahead of the session.
Multi-thread to the CIO now, not only the CFO, since technical/security/Oracle-integration questions are expected from that seat and Zycus currently has zero prior CIO contact.
Use the 13 Jul 2026 meeting to multi-thread directly into the CFO and CIO relationship rather than continuing to rely solely on the champion as the single thread into the committee.
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage action is closing the implementation-credibility gap before 13 Jul 2026; everything else is in reasonably good shape.

customer_preference.evidence: Champion Nishan Kumar directly relayed panel feedback on 2 Jul 2026: 'your presentation was very good... you got number one' on product and technology, and separately that Zycus is 'coming 1st on product assessment' among the 3 finalists.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of 3 remaining finalists (down from roughly 20) and is currently rated #1 on both product and technology by the buyer's technical committee. The single live threat is not a named product rival but the implementation-credibility gap: whichever finalist can show real India banking implementation experience has an edge, and GEP Worldwide is the most consistently cited comparator alongside Zycus in the buyer's own materials, making it the best current candidate for that rival, though this is an inference rather than a confirmed fact.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-qualified, buyer-preferred deal (ranked #1 on product and technology among 3 remaining finalists) that is genuinely at risk of losing on a single, fixable credibility gap: no India banking implementation track record, with the deciding CFO/CIO session only days away and the current close date already in the past.

deal_risk.text: Recorded close date (8 Jul 2026) has already passed and predates the decisive CFO meeting by 5 days, making the current forecast date unmeetable.
No India BFSI implementation partner is named yet, and the buyer has explicitly said this is what could cost Zycus the award despite leading on product.

win_position.text: Ranked #1 on product and technology — the champion relayed direct panel feedback on 2 Jul 2026 that Zycus outscored the field on both criteria.
Strong, active champion — Nishan Kumar is coaching Zycus on exactly how to win the CFO over and personally arranged the second, higher-stakes session.
Sole scoring gap is real and named — no India BFSI implementation experience was the only red flag raised by the panel (2 Jul 2026), and no partner has been named yet.
Economic buyer still unengaged — the CFO and CIO have had zero direct contact with Zycus; first contact is 13 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Escalating engagement — the process moved from a technical committee demo (17 Jun 2026) to a direct, buyer-initiated CFO/CIO session confirmed for 13 Jul 2026.
Buyer confirmed the meeting in writing on 8 Jul 2026, one day ago, keeping the timeline concrete.
Commercial and partner deliverables (ROI figures, named implementation partner) are still open with only days of runway before the CFO meeting.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer offered Zycus a second, more senior session rather than eliminating it, explicitly to let it address the implementation-experience gap (2 Jul 2026).
Budget described as open by the buyer's own team (10 Apr 2026 call), removing price as the primary blocker.
